mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are positive in >55% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and consumer prices indicate robust demand outpacing supply constraints, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both rise, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly increases corporate output and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week, but only during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during monetary tightening cycles, producing negative forward returns in >50% of cases when FEDFUNDS rises by >0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher aggregate demand, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts surpass 1.2 million units, it signals a broad-based recovery in construction and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1200.0, or the threshold loses predictive significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while inflation remains elevated, real interest rates increase and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >55% of high-rate-high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter, but only when unemployment remains below 6%.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when UNRATE exceeds 6%, with forward returns turning negative in >50% of cases during high unemployment periods.
